title: Codex Discoveries by Region
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM CodexEntry WHERE IsNewEntry = 1 AND event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate
detailQuery: SELECT COALESCE(Region, 'Unknown') AS label, COUNT(*) AS value FROM CodexEntry WHERE IsNewEntry = 1 AND event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate GROUP BY Region ORDER BY value DESC

details.text: {total} new codex discovery(ies) recorded during the report period. The galactic region breakdown shows where your discoveries clustered — whether in the familiar Orion Spur, the distant Sagittarius-Carina arm, or the far reaches of the Outer Arm where few commanders have catalogued anything at all.
details.help: CodexEntry Region is the galactic region of the discovery. total drives the title. Regions include Orion-Cygnus Arm, Sagittarius-Carina Arm, etc.

tagLines: Codex discovery galactic region distribution compiled. New entry origin map noted in the discovery record.
Different regions hide different things. The distribution shows which sectors you've been exploring.
The Codex gets thinner the further from the bubble you go. You pushed into the thin parts.
